title: Francis L. Racine
content:
People.ContactEmail flracine@mccarthy.ca
Francis L. Racine is an associate in our Labor and Employment and Pensions, Benefits and Executive Compensation groups. Based in Montreal, he represents and advises employers on a variety of strategic issues, including executive compensation, employment contracts, labour standards, labour relations management, incentive compensation plans, terms and conditions of employment and collective agreement negotiations and application, human rights and accommodation, occupational health and safety, pay equity, application of the Charter of the French Language, employee benefit plans, access to information and protection of privacy and personal information in the employment context.
Francis focuses his practice and has in-depth knowledge and experience in employment and employee benefits, more particularly in the context of Canadian and cross-border business transactions, as well as insolvency and bankruptcy, including mergers and acquisitions, financings, arrangements and restructurings. He routinely acts as lead counsel for buyers, sellers, insurers, debtors, creditors, lenders, monitors, trustees and receivers on strategic and sensitive employment, labour, benefits and pension issues.
Francis represents and defends local, national and international employers before Quebec administrative authorities in connection with investigations or formal notices, as well as before Quebec judicial and quasi-judicial bodies in litigation and settlement negotiations. He also assists employers with internal investigations in his areas of practice and regularly advises them on various compliance and adequation issues under Quebec and Canadian employment and benefits legislation, including pension plans.
Prior to joining McCarthy Tétrault, Francis was a senior counsel with a large national law firm for almost ten years. He also provides pro bono advice in his areas of practice to numerous not-for-profit organizations in Quebec and other provinces, as well as abroad.
